evals.prompt: I have a CSV of city eviction filings. Can you set up a repo for it?
evals.expected_output: Infers a low level (L0/L1), states the assumption and offers to climb. After a short interview, scaffolds a SMALL tree (README, AGENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, .gitignore/.gitattributes, data/{raw,interim,processed} with .gitkeep, DATA-DICTIONARY.md, decision-log.md, CHANGELOG.md). Does NOT emit a pipeline, governance, or OKF bundle. ROADMAP.md lists deferred higher-level concerns. No unfilled {{ }} tokens remain.

evals.prompt: Set up a reproducible Python project for analyzing eviction data, with a pipeline that re-runs when the data is refreshed.
evals.expected_output: Infers L2. Scaffolds the L0/L1 set plus the Python reproducible stack: environment.yml, Snakefile + config.yaml at the repo root (the single-pipeline default; a repo hosting several liberated pipelines would instead give each its own pipelines/<name>/), pyproject.toml, pre-commit config, exploratory/ for notebooks (NOTEBOOKS flag on → nbstripout git filter + AGENTS note). README includes the conda + snakemake run instructions (PIPELINE block kept). Offers to climb to L3 (collaboration) on completion.

evals.prompt: We're starting a multi-partner project with sensitive tenant records across three housing nonprofits. Help me set up the repository.
evals.expected_output: Infers L3+ and marks sensitivity = sensitive-human and multi-org. Generates the built L3 collaboration/governance set, with the governance docs grouped under docs/governance/ (GOVERNANCE.md — access tiers + retention + remedy/escalation + the values spine; CHARTER.md — purpose, partners, the roles table, and the collaboration protocol) and the community-health files at the repo root (CONTRIBUTING.md, which also carries how-we-track-work; CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md; CODEOWNERS). Does NOT emit separate ROLES.md, collaboration-protocol.md, INSTALLED-BASE.md, or PROJECT-MANAGEMENT.md files. Fires the affordance-duty coupling: identifier/retention/audit affordances ship with the matching access-tier and contestable-governance language in docs/governance/GOVERNANCE.md. Names design partners vs. beneficiaries separately and flags role gaps in CHARTER's gap-check. ROADMAP.md holds only genuinely deferred concerns; the responsible-data checklist (docs/checklists/) is recommended before any data ingestion.

evals.prompt: Audit this repository against open, reproducible, and inclusive data standards and tell me what's missing.
evals.expected_output: Enters Audit mode (read-only). Scores the repo against levels L0-L5 and the six goals, prints a prioritized gap table (Goal/Level -> Found? -> Recommended template/practice -> Source), checks OKF conformance if a knowledge/ bundle exists, and offers to scaffold the missing rungs without modifying the repo until approved.

evals.prompt: We're publishing this eviction dataset and its helper skills so other agents can find and use them. Set up agent discovery.
evals.expected_output: Infers L5 (publish) and sets the ARD flag because the project exposes agentic resources (nested .skills/ and a published dataset). Generates a repo-scope ai-catalog.json advertising the project's resources (Markdown skills as application/ai-skill+md, the dataset) with domain-anchored URNs (urn:ai:<domain>:skills:<slug>), a host block, capabilities and 2-5 representativeQueries, plus a DISCOVERY.md companion. Pairs it with the OKF knowledge bundle. Defers serving the manifest at the host /.well-known/ai-catalog.json path and registering with an org/ecosystem registry into ROADMAP.md. specVersion is "1.0"; ai-catalog.json is valid JSON and no unfilled {{ }} tokens remain.

evals.prompt: We've finished the analysis. Deposit the processed data, code, and documentation to Harvard Dataverse so we get a DOI.
evals.expected_output: Enters Deposit mode at L5 via data-project-depositor. Builds dataverse/dataset.json (citation block: title, author, datasetContact with email, dsDescription, subject from the controlled vocabulary). Runs the capability chain: pyDataverse if importable, else the curl deposit-dataverse.sh, else emits the kit (dataset.json + both scripts + DEPOSIT.md) and reports the one command plus the DATAVERSE_API_TOKEN and collection to set. Maps data/processed -> category Data, src/+pipeline -> Code, README/DATA-DICTIONARY/data-card/knowledge -> Documentation (categories + directoryLabel). Recommends a DRY_RUN against demo.dataverse.org. Creates a DRAFT and only publishes (mints the DOI) after explicit confirmation. If data is sensitive, gates on GOVERNANCE.md access tiers first (restrict/desensitize/withhold) and does not deposit identifiable traces openly. Idempotent via .dataverse-deposit.json.

evals.prompt: We deposit this dataset to Harvard Dataverse and refresh it every quarter. Set that up.
evals.expected_output: Infers L5 deposit with periodic updates: sets DATAVERSE + UPDATING, UPDATE_FREQUENCY=Quarterly, and DEPOSIT_CRON to a quarterly schedule. Emits the dataverse deposit kit plus templates/ci/dataverse-deposit.yml (schedule + workflow_dispatch) that re-deposits a DRAFT new version with --no-publish and opens a review issue, never auto-publishing. dataset.json carries notesText (update frequency) and timePeriodCovered; DEPOSIT.md and the L4 data-management-plan explain one DOI + a new version per refresh, cited by version + UNF. Notes the DATAVERSE_API_TOKEN repository secret the scheduled workflow needs. No unfilled tokens remain.

evals.prompt: We want to share our processed datasets and our agent skills with a partner organization without giving them a copy of the data. Wire up OpenSharing.
evals.expected_output: Infers L5 and sets the OPENSHARING flag. Emits the opensharing/ kit: share.json declaring a Share (named from the project slug) with a 'processed' Schema that maps data/processed -> Table assets (format + object-store location + accessModes), file/doc directories -> Volume assets (storageLocation), and .skills/ -> AgentSkill assets; share-profile.example.json (the recipient Delta-Sharing profile: shareCredentialsVersion, endpoint, bearerToken placeholder); and SHARING.md covering serve (run an OpenSharing/Delta-Sharing server over object storage that vends temporary scoped credentials) and consume (delta-sharing client). Stresses sharing is zero-copy/in-place and that the skill emits only the declaration -- it does not run a server or vend credentials (deferred to ROADMAP/SHARING.md). Gates what enters a share on GOVERNANCE.md access tiers (scope/desensitize/withhold for sensitive data) and gitignores the filled share-profile.json because the bearer token is a secret. Notes the AgentSkill assets are the same .skills/ the ARD ai-catalog advertises. share.json is valid JSON with no unfilled {{ }} tokens.

evals.prompt: We're liberating four different data sources into one repo, each with its own pipeline. Set up CI that tests all of them, and make sure adding a fifth later doesn't mean editing the workflow.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes a multi-pipeline monorepo and sets the PIPELINES flag. Scaffolds one self-contained pipelines/<name>/ per source (Snakefile, config.yaml, environment.yml, README.md) and emits templates/ci/pipelines-ci.yml (the auto-discovered matrix) INSTEAD of the single-project github-actions-ci.yml. The workflow has a discover job that globs pipelines/*/Snakefile into a JSON array and a test job that fans out via a matrix over fromJSON(needs.discover.outputs.pipelines) with a per-pipeline working-directory, plus the always-on unfilled-token guard job; fail-fast is false so one red pipeline doesn't cancel the others. Adding a fifth pipeline needs no workflow edit (it is discovered on the next run). Points at references/landing-a-pipeline.md for the landing procedure and references/stamping-and-shared-core.md for factoring a shared core at ~3 pipelines. No unfilled {{ }} tokens remain (GitHub Actions ${{ }} expressions are allowed in the workflow).

evals.prompt: We've built the snowpack pipeline on a branch. Land it in our multi-pipeline repo and close its tracking issue.
evals.expected_output: Follows the ordered landing procedure from references/landing-a-pipeline.md: works in an isolated worktree/branch off an up-to-date origin/<default-branch>; scaffolds a self-contained pipelines/snowpack/ (stamped from a sibling over the shared core per references/stamping-and-shared-core.md, not copy-pasted wholesale); relies on the auto-discovered CI matrix, or — if the matrix is static — adds the snowpack row in the SAME PR as the directory; registers the pipeline across the repo docs (README, ROADMAP, CHANGELOG, AGENTS, decision-log, data-management-plan, data-card, wiki, both CI workflows, engagement-sync.json) and drops a <!-- data-project:pipeline=snowpack --> marker; opens ONE PR titled 'Add snowpack pipeline (#NN)' with 'Closes #NN' and nothing else (never bundled into a docs/governance PR); merges only when CI is green; then git fetch and verifies origin/<default-branch>. The data-project-tracker closes the issue from the merged PR rather than a roadmap edit, and reconciles pipelines/* against the documented pipelines, flagging any built-but-open or under-registered mismatch.

evals.prompt: Take this project to L4. I don't want two dozen files dumped in the repo root.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolds L3/L4 but keeps the root uncluttered. Governance, the charter, and the data-management plan land under docs/governance/; the responsible-data, bulletproofing, data-quality, and accessibility checklists under docs/checklists/; the data card under docs/. The GitHub community-health files (CONTRIBUTING, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, CODEOWNERS) and the active working docs (README, AGENTS, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP, decision-log, DATA-DICTIONARY) stay at the repo root. No separate INSTALLED-BASE.md, ROLES.md, collaboration-protocol.md, PROJECT-MANAGEMENT.md, or contributed-data-intake.md is created — the values spine folds into GOVERNANCE, roles + the collaboration protocol into CHARTER, how-we-track-work into CONTRIBUTING, and contributed-data intake into the data-management plan. AGENTS.md indexes the docs/ tree and points at each governance doc at its decision point, so the generated docs are wired into agent processes rather than left inert. No unfilled {{ }} tokens remain.
